summary: High‑energy spring ’91 show with standout Truckin’ > Smokestack, Watchtower and Foolish Heart; soundboard source is generally crisp despite occasional static glitches.
review: The April 3, 1991 Atlanta concert captures a vibrant performance – the second set brims with energy and features double‑Jerry gems like Foolish > Crazy, while the first set delivers classics such as Truckin’ > Smokestack Lightning and a soaring Watchtower. The SBD recording is mostly clear and “top‑notch,” though a few listeners note static during TMNS and a brief glitch on GSET; these blemishes don’t detract from overall listenability.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (9 out of 11) rate the show 4‑5 stars and praise both performance and recording; only one review is strongly negative about audio quality, so the weighted average lands in the strong‑good range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp SBD with clear vocals and instruments. Minor issues: static during "The Music Never Stopped" (TMNS), a brief glitch on GSET, occasional cloudiness noted by one reviewer.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set solid and lengthy; second set especially energetic with tight transitions and a double‑Jerry highlight (Foolish > Crazy). Overall flow described as “interesting” and “well‑executed.”

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for perfect tone, soaring vocals and the "double Jerry gem" of Foolish>Crazy.
band_member_comments.Keys: Great keys noted; Brent (Bobby) praised alongside Vince, Billy and Mickey for being in tune and delivering generous spirit.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy & Mickey mentioned as tight and in‑tune throughout the show.
